title: World document
description: One star system (star, planets, stations), loaded at server startup. Derived from the Gleam decoders in server/src/dh_server/world.gleam (world_decoder/station_decoder/body_decoder/commodity_decoder/market_entry_decoder/orbit_decoder) and server/src/dh_server/deckplan.gleam (decoder) — those decoders are the source of truth; this schema is documentation and a typo-catcher, not a substitute for them. additionalProperties is false at every object level even though the decoders themselves ignore unknown fields, precisely so this schema can catch typos the decoders would silently swallow. Note: every field typed 'number' here backs a Gleam decode.float field, which REJECTS bare JSON integers (e.g. 5 is invalid, 5.0 is required) — JSON Schema draft 2020-12 cannot distinguish 1 from 1.0, so that constraint is documented here rather than enforced.

spawn_station.description: Must reference an existing station id (enforced by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema).

radius.description: decode.float: JSON value must have a decimal point (e.g. 4000.0), a bare integer is rejected.

period_s.description: decode.float: JSON value must have a decimal point, a bare integer is rejected.

phase.description: decode.float: JSON value must have a decimal point, a bare integer is rejected.

kind.description: Free-form in the decoder (no enum, e.g. "star"/"planet" are conventions, not constraints); left open here.

parent.description: decode.field + decode.optional: the key is required, but its value may be null (no parent, e.g. the star) or a body id string.

orbit.description: decode.field + decode.optional: the key is required, but its value may be null (no orbit, e.g. the star) or an orbit object.

radius.description: decode.float: JSON value must have a decimal point, a bare integer is rejected.

mu.description: decode.float: JSON value must have a decimal point, a bare integer is rejected.

commodity.description: Must reference an existing commodity id (enforced by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema).

station.description: A world station instance: per-instance placement/economy plus a `class` reference (issue #30). dock_radius/crane/concourse come from the referenced station class, and berths derive from that concourse's Q glyphs (issue #31) — none of them live here.

class.description: References a station class id under server/stationclasses/ (resolved by the Gleam decoder; an unknown id is rejected there, not by this schema).

parent.description: Must reference an existing body id (enforced by the Gleam decoder's validate step, not by this schema).

market.description: Optional field (decode.optional_field, default []). Per-instance trade terms.

grid.description: 3*height rows of 3*width chars: each tile is a 3x3 block (centre = floor/void/stairs; N/E/S/W edge-mids = wall/door/fixture). See docs/deckplan-format.md.

kind.description: Free-form in the decoder (e.g. "helm"/"cargo"/"broker" are conventions, not constraints); left open here.

deck.description: Which deck index this console sits on (default 0).
Which deck arriving characters appear on (default 0).

tile.description: [x, y] tile where arriving characters appear.

consoles.description: Optional. Authored as center glyphs (h/c/b/Q) and derived at load; the server re-emits it (namespaced) over the wire.
